goal: Port structural.deps.cycles to Structure.dependency_graph; retire deps.py

summary.title: Port structural.deps.cycles to Structure; retire deps.py
summary.what: Adds Structure.dependency_cycles() (Tarjan SCC over Structure.dependency_graph from Intent A) and detect_cycles helper in slop.structure._imports. Rewrites slop.structure.rules.dependencies.run_cycles to consume Structure (signature change from Path-first to Structure-first). Drops legacy_v2_shim wrapper. Deletes slop/_structural/deps.py (728 lines). Migrates 6 test files (test_dependencies plus 4 per-language deps tests) and adds 3 cycle-detection unit tests on Structure.
summary.why: deps_kernel was the last consumer of legacy import-graph code after Intents A/B. The substrate-native dependency_graph was already in place; only Tarjan SCC and a rule rewire were needed to retire deps.py. Keeping deps.py alive would have preserved the legacy regex fallback + target-mode + max_results surface without any current consumer, plus duplicate the import-extraction logic that already lives in Structure.imports.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.deps.cycles to Structure.dependency_graph; retire deps.py

decisions.rationale: The legacy implementation is recursive. For typical codebases (a few thousand files) Python's default recursion limit (1000) is comfortable. Switching to explicit-stack iteration would be more robust but adds complexity without an observed need.

decisions.chose: Dropped legacy-only test behaviours (target mode, max_results, regex fallback)
decisions.rationale: These were features of deps_kernel that no rule consumed and that don't fit the substrate model. Target mode was a kernel-level focus parameter; max_results was a kernel cap; regex fallback was already documented as a parity break in Intent A. Carrying the tests forward would have required reimplementing functionality with no consumer.

decisions.chose: Cycle detection stays in _imports.py (not a separate _cycles.py)
decisions.rationale: Tarjan SCC operates directly on the dependency graph. Splitting into a separate helper module would add an import without adding clarity. _imports.py is now the home for everything graph-shape on Structure (extract_imports + build_dependency_graph + detect_cycles).

review_notes: CLI smoke test on slop source surfaces two cycles. One is a true positive (linter format/result cycle, pre-existing); one is a TYPE_CHECKING false positive in Structure helpers (substrate sees the import statement but doesn't know it's guarded). Both are out-of-scope for this intent.
Per-language deps tests (c/cpp/julia/ruby) were updated to pass Structure via inline Tree.scan() — a small ceremony that matches every other view-native rule test in the repo.
All legacy callers retired. 'grep -rln "deps_kernel\|_structural.deps" src/' now returns zero hits.

architectural_claims: Cycle detection is substrate-aligned: Structure.dependency_cycles() consumes the same DependencyGraph that powers Structure.packages — single source of truth for the import graph.
All Intent-A/B/C work shares one extraction pipeline (Language.import_queries → Structure.imports → DependencyGraph → cycles/packages). No legacy kernel remains in the import-graph path.
behavioral_changes: run_cycles signature changes from (root, rc, sc) to (structure, rc, sc).
structural.deps rule no longer reports 'errors' in summary — the substrate-native path uses Tree.scan's own error handling and doesn't expose extraction errors at the cycle layer.
Regex text fallback for files that fail to parse is gone (matches Intent A's documented parity break).
migration_notes: Custom rules importing from slop._structural.deps will break. The substrate replacements are slop.structure.records.Import, slop.structure.records.DependencyGraph, and slop.structure.view.Structure.{imports, dependency_graph, dependency_cycles}.
